text:
The article explores the development of the institution and the structure of the Kyiv Theological Academy and examines its role as one of the most important institutions of spiritual education, scientific research and cultural development in Ukraine and the Russian Empire. The Academy was considered to be a major personnel forge, which continuously produced highly qualified specialists who occupied key educational and scientific state and local government positions in the church and state structures.

The Research paper presents the historical and educational context of the Academy within the system of educational reforms implemented in the Russian Empire, which formed the vertical of spiritual education, with Theological Academies serving as the Higher Education institutions for each of the four spiritual and educational districts established in Kyiv, Kazan, Moscow and St. Petersburg. Essential to understanding the development of the Academy is the significance of the official Orthod. The official Orthodox Theological Academy charters of 1814, 1869, 1884 and 1910 determined such issues as staffing, student numbers, as well as the requirements for appointing teachers and for granting degrees and academic ranks to students.

The paper also describes the government of the Academy through its collegiate organs: the Academic Conference, and the internal and external (district) Boards with administrative authority. Finally, the study turns to the leadership of the senior management group – that of the rector and the inspector – and illustrates their activities and their vertical relations with the higher bureaucratic authorities in the imperial system of higher education.
